spec_match.rationale: Compares old dialog (top frame only) vs new dialog (top frame + iframe origin) across legit-embed, phishing-typo, and other scenarios — directly maps to the spec PR's motivation of helping users notice hijacked iframes.

interactive.rationale: Scenario selector drives a side-by-side old/new dialog mock with a verdict text per scenario; well above static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb sells 'pick a real-world scenario and watch the difference between the old dialog and the new one' — page delivers that with multiple scenario presets.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes legit-payment, phishing-typo, and other scenarios across the iframeIsEvil / idpIsEvil axis. Covers the headline cases the spec PR uses to motivate the change.

references.rationale: The see-also list now includes the required ChromeStatus feature 5176474637959168 alongside the FedCM issue and spec PR.

design_system.rationale: Side-by-side dialog mocks use --bg-paper / --border-black; verdict text uses --accent-emerald (good signal) / --accent-rose (bad signal); no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'compromised legitimate iframe' scenario
openQuestions.detail: The current scenarios are all 'evil iframe origin' or 'honest iframe origin'. A more nuanced case is 'legitimate iframe origin compromised post-load' — the dialog still shows a 'real' origin but the script has been tampered. The disclosure can't help here; document that limit explicitly so the demo doesn't oversell the defence.

openQuestions.title: Surface the user-action data the dialog logs
openQuestions.detail: FedCM dialogs are intended to drive measurable user choices. A small 'what the user did' counter (Continue / Cancel / closed dialog) keyed by scenario would let authors reason about the signal quality of the new disclosure across honest-vs-phishing populations.

summary: Strong scenario-driven comparator that nails the spec's motivation and now includes the canonical ChromeStatus link. Remaining follow-ups are a compromised-legitimate-iframe case and user-action signal tracking.
